title: potentilla reptans subvar. glabra
intro: Potentilla reptans subvar. glabra, also known as cinquefoil, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the rose family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and tannins. The medicinal properties of Potentilla reptans subvar. glabra make it beneficial for treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers, as well as for reducing fever and alleviating skin irritations. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried extract, which are commonly used to harness its health benefits and medicinal properties.
